description: init_payment_gateway uses std::call_once for race-safe one-time initialisation; the other two initialisers use the naive `if (!ptr) ptr = make_unique(...)` pattern which is racy under concurrent first use. Unifying the three onto a shared lazy-init helper that drops call_once re-introduces a data race that crashes on double-init.

findings.rationale: init_payment_gateway calls std::call_once with a lambda; the other two initialisers branch on `if (!ptr)` and assign inside. Completely different control-flow shapes — likely Jaccard floor or structural-unclassified, since the divergent has no BRANCH and the siblings do.
